title: Sterculia campanulata
intro: Sterculia campanulata, also known as the African star apple, is a deciduous tree species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its showy, white flowers and edible fruits. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Sterculia campanulata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used to make herbal teas and tinctures.
taxonomy: Sterculia campanulata is classified within the family Malvaceae. This plant belongs to the order Malvales and the clade Rosids. At the family level, it is a member of the subfamily Sterculioideae. The genus Sterculia itself is part of the tribe Sterculieae. This classification helps to group Sterculia campanulata with its closely related species and understand its evolutionary relationships.
uses: Sterculia campanulata has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian countries for its medicinal properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be beneficial in treating various health issues. The plant's extracts have been used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Additionally, Sterculia campanulata is also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and as an antiseptic. However, further research is required to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
